channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Harrison Ford addresses AI fears
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to set your preferred web browser on your desktop and mobile devices
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy takes you through the steps to easily set your preferred web browser whether you're using a Mac, Windows PC, iPhone or Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Is your phone listening to everything you say? It’s complicated
item.description: Google, Instagram, Facebook and weather apps can track your location and use that data to serve you ads; here's what you can do to protect your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arizona advances bill to keep AI from rejecting medical claims
item.description: Arizona state lawmakers advanced a bill Thursday that would ban the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to deny medical claims. The Arizona House of Representatives passed the legislation 58-0, with two lawmakers sitting out the vote, and it now heads to the state senate for consideration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meze Audio's beautiful new wired headphones have a new kind of planar magnetic driver, hand-finished in Ukraine
item.description: These new premium headphones from Meze Audio go big on planar magnetic drivers, with a suitably big price.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple terminates UK data protection after government overreach
item.description: As of 10 a.m. (ET) today, Apple is no longer offering new users in the UK the chance to enable Advanced Data Protection. Essentially, anyone who now wants to enable the feature to protect their data against criminal or state surveillance or exfiltration will no longer be able to do so.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation
item.description: Leo Garciga, the Army’s CIO, said the Unified Network Optimization initiative is helping the service cut costs and duplicative applications. The post Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Apollo GraphQL ships connectors for REST APIs
item.description: Apollo GraphQL, provider of the GraphOS API orchestration platform, has introduced Apollo Connectors for REST APIs, a way to integrate REST APIs into a composable GraphQL architecture without writing boilerplate code. The company also announced GraphOS platform enhancements.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: CISA on Thin Ice: Trump's Dismantling of Federal Agencies Leaves Election Security in Jeopardy
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) faces an uncertain future as the Trump administration continues to dismantle federal agencies, leaving election security vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Huge data leak exposes 14 million customer shipping records
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Hipshipper, which is used by eBay, Shopify and Amazon sellers, exposed millions of shipping labels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defense tech company True Anomaly expands into California
item.description: True Anomaly, a defense technology company, announced its expansion into Southern California with a 90,000-square-foot factory in Long Beach.
item.dc:creator: Christina Merino, The Mercury News

item.title: Fortinet speeds threat detection with improved FortiAnalyzer
item.description: Fortinet has reinforced its FortiAnalyzer security analytics and log management platform with capabilities that enhance enterprise threat detection and automate incident response. FortiAnalyzer securely collects logs from Fortinet devices such as FortiGate firewalls and endpoint security agents.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Smart home device maker exposes 2.7 billion records in huge data breach
item.description: Major IoT data breach: Mars Hydro database with 2.7 billion records exposed. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the breach is a reminder of the risks that come with using IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Generative AI vs. the software developer
item.description: If you aren’t aware of the tectonic shift that is generative artificial intelligence, then I can only assume your phone has been on Airplane mode for the last year. You can’t swing a laptop bag these days without knocking over four startups doing something with AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data center spending to top $1 trillion by 2029 as AI transforms infrastructure
item.description: Massive global demand for AI technology is causing data centers to increase spending on servers, power, and cooling infrastructure. As a result, data center CapEx spending will hit $1.1 trillion by 2029, up from $430 billion in 2024, according to Dell’Oro Group. Much of this growth is due to AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Key strategies for MLops success in 2025
item.description: Integrating and managing artificial intelligence and machine learning effectively within business operations has become a top priority for businesses looking to stay competitive in an ever evolving landscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How to keep AI hallucinations out of your code
item.description: It turns out androids do dream, and their dreams are often strange. In the early days of generative AI, we got human hands with eight fingers and recipes for making pizza sauce from glue. Now, developers working with AI-assisted coding tools are also finding AI hallucinations in their code.
item.dc:creator: Info World
